title: Volumes, PersistentVolumes, Claims, StorageClasses, CSI, Snapshots, and Recovery · Stakeholder Platform Mission
summary: A nonprofit intake operations team. The team needs identity-bound stateful workloads, topology-aware claims, safe reclaim, application-consistent backup, clean-cluster restore, checksum evidence, and recovery-objective defense. Planned artifact: Deliver Recoverable Stateful Intake Platform with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design StatefulSet ordinal identity, headless discovery, stable claims, ordering, update partition, scaling, and failure recovery around application guarantees.
Design StorageClasses with provisioner, parameters, defaulting, reclaim policy, expansion, mount options, and WaitForFirstConsumer topology behavior.
Explain CSI controller and node roles, drivers, snapshots, clones, expansion, topology, credentials, feature support, and deprecated in-tree migration boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project K8s Statefulset Identity Storage
steps.instruction:
project case — A project StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Design StatefulSet ordinal identity, headless discovery, stable claims, ordering, update partition, scaling, and failure recovery around application guarantees.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-app-consistent-backup-restore-138llb
  - competency: k8s-app-consistent-backup-restore
    artifact: storage-recovery.yaml
    decision: app consistent backup restore
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: server-side diff managed fields and admission decision
    case-key: 138llb
    change: |
      claim: intake-data
      storageClass: retained-zonal
      volumeBindingMode: WaitForFirstConsumer
      reclaimPolicy: Retain
      snapshot: application-quiesced
      restore: fresh-namespace-and-claim
      checksum: verified
    verify: kubectl get pv,pvc,storageclass,volumesnapshot -A -o json
    failure: Reject Bound as durability proof, Delete reclaim surprises, arbitrary-zone binding, snapshot-as-backup claims, or restore without application validation.
    repair: Trace claim and volume lifecycle, align topology and access, quiesce the application, protect metadata and keys, and rehearse clean restore.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A Bound PVC and successful VolumeSnapshot prove the application can recover within its objectives.
